title: SuggestionReported payload
description: Emitted when a new suggestion is filed via forge(schema_name='suggestion', ...). Captures the create-time field values; mirrors the suggestion forge-schema. Subsequent mutations emit their own typed events (SuggestionResolved, SuggestionReopened, SuggestionStamped). The suggestion's entity reference is in the envelope; this schema covers payload-only. NOTE: native vocabulary throughout — `priority` not `severity`. See chain `agent-suggestion-box` for why suggestions are a separate entity from bugs rather than a discriminator on the bugs table.

title.description: Short human description — one line, sentence-style. Mirrors suggestion.toml's title field. Required at file-time.

problem_statement.description: What the suggestion proposes and why it would help. Mirrors suggestion.toml's problem_statement — forward-looking voice (the case for the change), unlike bug.problem_statement which is observational.

surface.description: Subsystem / crate / concept the suggestion lives in. Comma-separated lowercase-kebab tags. Convention matches suggestion.toml — see its description for the matching semantics in suggestion_list.

priority.description: Accepted values: low, medium, high. null when the caller omitted priority; the forge writer defaults to medium downstream. Native vocabulary — this is the suggestion-side analogue of severity.

source.description: Where the suggestion was surfaced — conventional value 'session retro on <YYYY-MM-DD>' for skill-driven filings; originating session/arc identifier for arcreview-pipeline filings.

tags.description: Categorical labels — broader than bug.tags (testing, lint, docs, tooling, prose, architecture, skill, workflow). Comma-separated, lowercase-kebab.

acceptance_criteria.description: What 'adopted' looks like. Accepts a list of strings (one bullet per criterion) or a single string (coerced to a 1-element list upstream). Optional at file-time.

constraints.description: Scope limits — don't-over-build notes, boundaries the implementation must respect. Paragraph prose.
